entries.speaker: maren
lines:
Torren sent word through the spirits. Faster than
walking, though less polite.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Show me.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Where did you find it?

entries.speaker: edren
lines:
Beneath an Ironmouth mine. A pre-civilization ruin.
There were dead miners — no wounds. Their faces were—

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Frozen in despair. Yes. I've read about this.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
This thing has no power of its own. It's not a weapon,
not a source, not a tool.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Then what is it?

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
It's a conduit. A focus point for something beyond it.
Think of it as a handle on a door that's been sealed for a very
long time. And something on the other side knows you touched it.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: A door to what?

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
I don't know everything. I know this: the miners
didn't die from the Pendulum. They died from what's behind it.
Despair so deep it replaces the will to live. They sat down and
the world emptied out of them.

entries.speaker: torren
lines:
The spirits call it the Grey. Old word. Older than
the tribes.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
Different names in different ages. The Pallor. The
Grey. The Weight. It doesn't matter what you call it. It's been
on the other side of that door for a very long time, and now it
knows someone touched the handle.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Can it be destroyed?

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
I don't know. I know it can't be left here. The ley
line beneath this cottage is already dimming — I can feel it
responding to the Pendulum's presence. Wherever you take it, the
world around it will start to fade.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
Then we take it to people who can study it properly.
Valdris has resources — containment, ley researchers, the court
mages.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
The court mages will want to use it. The king will
want it as leverage against the Compact. And the thing behind
the door will let them think they're in control right up until
they aren't.

entries.speaker: sable
lines:
So we can't leave it, can't destroy it, and can't
give it to anyone. Wonderful.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
We can take it to people who understand the danger.
And I can be one of those people.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
I left the court because they wouldn't listen. Maybe
now they'll have to.

entries.speaker: torren
lines: You're coming with us?

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
I've spent years studying this in isolation. If that
door is opening, isolation isn't enough anymore.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
To Valdris, then. And pray they listen better than
they did last time.
